terms.definition: An object that produces values one at a time via __next__, raising StopIteration when there are no more.
A function that uses yield to produce a lazy iterator, pausing and resuming its state between values.
The keyword that suspends a generator, hands a value back to the caller, and resumes from the same point on the next request.
A statement that delegates to a sub-iterable, re-yielding all of its values from within a generator.
Computing values only when they are needed, rather than all at once, which saves memory and enables infinite/streamed sequences.
The exception an iterator raises to signal it is exhausted; for loops catch it automatically to end the loop.
